topic.what: Three assertions highlighting fundamental misunderstandings in NIDS research to reshape the field
topic.how: Rooted in established security principles with recommendations and reproducible case study
topic.why_matters: Research on NIDS is stagnant; findings not reflected in operational contexts; gap between research and real-world deployment needs foundation to address

characteristics.what_it_measures: Gap between original CICIDS17/18 citations vs fixed version citations
characteristics.evidence: Since 2022-2025, fixed versions [62,92] received 457 citations vs original [116] with 4578
characteristics.context: Shows research community overlooks dataset issues

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of alerts generated in real-world NIDS operations
characteristics.value: over 220k samples deemed malicious in a single working week
characteristics.evidence: Engelen et al. [62] dataset: 1.8M benign + 881k malicious NetFlows in one week
characteristics.context: Demonstrates alert fatigue problem - human operators cannot triage individual alerts

characteristics.what_it_measures: Mismatch between detected anomalies and actual security incidents
characteristics.evidence: Anomaly-based NIDS flag benign novel behavior as attacks while missing actual intrusions
characteristics.context: Key distinction between anomalies (statistical) and security-noteworthy events (semantic)
